observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. immediately derecognize from PPE accounts all items costing below ₱50,000.00 under the Machinery and Equipment and Furniture, Fixtures and Books accounts, and
2. coordinate with the Supply Officer in the immediate preparation of documents for the proper disposal of the unserviceable and destroyed properties after which derecognize the said properties, hence the affected accounts will be presented fairly in the financial statements.

The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of ₱1,072,867.98 from Trust Fund to the General Fund bank account and ensure that actual transfer of cash arising from adjustments or corrections of transactions is effected immediately, hence cash backing for issued check payments is available and possible confusion as to what bank account to charge such payment will be avoided.

The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct warranty security on small value procurements or
payments for supplies and materials delivered which are not for immediate
consumption or at least consumed within three months and require her to
release after the lapse of the warranty period or after consumption thereof.
Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
coordinate with each other in order to effect the release of the long overdue
warranty securities to supplies/dealers.

The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer require the contractors to secure approval from DOLE for their CSHP and observe strict compliance and implementation of the safety programs to ensure the protection and welfare of workers employed as well as the safety of the general public within the vicinity of the construction site during the contract implementation. It was further recommended that management refrain from entering into a contract with contractors who lack/cannot submit the approved CSHP.

The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold CVAT and EWT for all payments and require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the same to the BIR on or before due dates in compliance with the aforementioned BIR Revenue Order to avoid possible incurrence of penalties/surcharges and to raise additional funds for use of the National government.

The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure every RFQs clearly shows the ABC for the related procurement in compliance with RA 9184 and in order to strengthen its integrity over the process and to enable the municipality from obtaining the most advantageous price in its procurements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT and SET by the issuance of the notice of delinquency and post the same at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay concerned in order to have additional income to fund for programs and projects of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to prioritize the implementation of the projects funded out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) undertake
revenue-enhancing strategies and cost-cutting measures to address the cash deficit;
and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to regularly prepare the Daily Cash
Position Report to serve as guide in certifying fund availability and ensuring that all
disbursements are supported by actual cash; (c) direct the LFC to: (i) assess the target
revenues by examining the actual collections of previous years in the preparation of
the budget for succeeding years to come up with a viable and realistic budget; and
(ii) exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of the
Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and
collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the MT and the MA to conduct
regular reconciliation of their cash records to promptly identify discrepancies and
effect the necessary adjustments in their respective books; and (b) the MT to
continuously ensure the regular deposit of collections received from collecting officers
to safeguard government funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to sustain compliance with the
timely preparation of BRSs and the prompt adjustment of reconciling items in the
books to ensure the fair presentation of the CIB-LCCA account as of year-end.

We recommended that the LCE (a) establish an Inventory Committee tasked
with conducting a physical inventory of the Municipality's PPE and ensure that the
Committee members are temporarily relieved from their regular duties to complete
the undertaking and submit an updated RPCPPE to the MA and AT not later than
January 31 of each year; (b) require the MTO/GSO to: (i) coordinate with the MAO
to update subsidiary records and prioritize the completion of the inventory count and
reconciliation of PPE items; (ii) immediately request the disposal of unserviceable
PPEs to prevent further deterioration and decline in the commercial value of the
properties; and (iii) provide the MA with the IIRUP containing the list of
unserviceable PPE, as the basis for their derecognition in the books; and (c) require
the MA to derecognize from the books unserviceable PPEs with no future economic
benefits or service potential expected.

We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure the timely implementation of the
NGA-funded PPAs within the targeted timeframe for the promotion of the general
welfare of the Municipality’s constituents; (b) maintain compliance with all required
documentary and financial reporting obligations; and (c) ensure that any unutilized
funds are promptly returned to the source agencies upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, direct the LSB
Secretary to ensure the timely submission of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization
reports using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 to the DepEd Central Office,
SB, and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, as required under Item 6.1 of
JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE, as head of the Municipal Development
Council, (a) ensure continuous adherence to the 20% NTA allocation to finance the
developmental projects of the Municipality; (b) ensure that all PPAs to be funded out
of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement- and implementation-ready for the
attainment of the desired social-economic outcomes within the fiscal year; and
(c) ensure that developmental projects included under the 20% DF are necessary,
appropriate, and directly contribute to the promotion of the general welfare of the
constituents.

We recommended that the LCE (a) comply with the rules and regulations on the
appropriation for the LDRRMF based on the estimated revenue from regular
sources; (b) ensure optimal utilization of the Fund to mitigate the loss of lives and
properties in the event of natural disasters or calamities; (c) require the MA to
consistently adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC
No. 2013-1 regarding the transfer of the unexpended balance to ensure the availability
of LDRRMF for the implementation of other disaster risk reduction and management
activities of the Municipality; and (d) require the MDRRMO to ensure (i) that the
PPAs budgeted under the LDRRMF are well planned and implementation-ready to
mitigate, if not totally prevent, the potential risks and adverse impacts of calamities
or disasters; and (ii) continuous compliance with the timely submission of the Report
on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the AT on or before the 15th day after
the end of each month for review and verification.

We recommended that the LCE (a) assess the existing Organizational
Structure so that the ideal number of plantilla positions will be established and
prioritize filling up vacant plantilla positions to avoid excessive number of JO
personnel; and (b) adhere to the policies and guidelines in hiring of personnel under
JO status as provided in CSC Resolution No. 020790.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to
faithfully comply with the provisions of BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and
remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to (a) continue
ensuring timely deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations
including the government share within the prescribed period; and (b) coordinate
regularly with GSIS to reconcile employee records and resolve any discrepancies.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to (a) continuously
comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions
within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve the necessary records to determine the
details of the unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to (a) continuously
comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions
within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve the necessary records to determine the
details of the unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE require the persons responsible to
immediately settle the suspensions totaling ₱3,987,141.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately drop / derecognize from the PPE accounts all items costing below ₱15,000.00, items with no data available and the distributed items to farmers by preparing the necessary adjusting entries. Likewise, instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of the items dropped/derecognized and issue a corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant had already derecognized all items below P15,000.00. The Supply Officer had cancelled the PAR of the items and had prepared ICS for such.
The GSO complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create immediately an Inventory Committee to take charge in the physical inventory of all PPEs of the municipality. The Inventory Committee with the coordinated efforts of the Municipal Accountant, Supply Officer and other concerned offices shall comply with the guidelines and procedures enunciated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to address the issue of having recurring and enormous discrepancies in its PPE accounts and have a reliable PPE balance that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year. The Municipal Accountant remind those granted with cash advances to present their liquidation papers for obligation and refund any unexpended balance to close the account. Thus, the expenses for the year are properly presented/accounted. In the case of the four cash advances, adjust the entry made to restate the expenses for 2023.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct every requisitioning unit / department to prepare PR accompanied by a duly accomplished ObR to be certified by the Municipal Budget Office to ensure the existence of an appropriation and in compliance with the aforementioned circular. Likewise, direct the BAC to make sure that ObR is an attachment to every procurement that has to be undertaken by the municipal government.

The BAC complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to compute penalty on the RPT/SET due for the year and stop granting of discount on the quarters allegedly subjected to installment basis. In this manner, under-collection of RPT/SET could be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had computed properly the RPT and SET.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, determine the priority for project/s to be funded out from the savings of RA 7171 and include the projects in the formulation of the Local Development Investment Programs and Annual Investment Program and request the legislative body for the appropriation of such. Thus, the objectives of RA 7171 would be achieved at its maximum.

observations.recommendation: 1.10 In order to clean the books and to fairly present the receivable accounts in the financial statements, we recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to establish allowance for impairment of the uncollectible receivable accounts, and we reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that secure all the requisite documents for request for write-off, hence affected accounts will be fairly presented in the Statement of Financial Position.

2.4 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer of the Municipality, and the LGUs Appraisal Committee to facilitate the proper disposal of the unserviceable properties to enable the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to drop from the books the corresponding cost of these properties and to have additional income from the saleable scrap items, thus ensuring accuracy and propriety of reported PPE in the financial statements of the Municipal Government.

3.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
3.7.1 Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copies of the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) of the above-mentioned on-going and completed projects as basis for the recording of construction in progress and payable accounts; and
3.7.2 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to correct the overstatement of Construction in Progress- Infrastructure Assets and the understatement of the related PPE account and Accounts Payable, in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the Financial statements.

4.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all her collections to the authorized government depository bank daily or the next banking day, pursuant to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS in order to avoid holding of large amount of cash in the vault and prevent possible risk of loss or misuse of funds.

5.4 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, through its BAC Secretariat, send the Notification of Bid Submission and let the bidder receive the documents to ensure transparency and to establish that the bidder is the one who actually submitted the bid proposal and not a dummy.

6.6 We recommended that the LCE being the head of the Local Government of San Ildefonso, make sure that all programs and projects implemented by the municipality are properly and faithfully implemented. He must instruct all the department heads in charge on the implementation of projects to determine only qualified beneficiaries and not to favor selected individuals only. Likewise, truthful and accurate list of recipients must be duly prepared and accomplished to support any distribution to avoid any suspicion and attain transparency in any government transaction.

7.2 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to post in the PhilGEPS website all awards for alternative modes of Procurement above P 50,000.00 and attach the copies of printouts as supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers to manifest and attain transparency and efficiency in the procurement.

8.4 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to deduct retention money from payment to supplier or require the latter to post special bank guarantee as prescribed by law in order to protect the interest of the municipality in the event that manufacturing defect occurs.

9.3 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Supply Officer and Inspectorate Committee to properly accomplish and fill in the required dates and information in the PO and IAR in order that the same will be considered by the Municipal Accountant, thus penalties due to the delayed deliveries could be imposed and deducted from the payment to the supplier. Likewise, require the Supply Officer to ensure that DR and/or sales invoice must be properly dated to corroborate the IAR to determine whether the supplier have complied with the terms of the contract and deliver the goods within the period stipulated in the PO/Contract.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and end-users only indicate the specification in the Purchase Request and Requests for Quotations in order to give chance to all qualified suppliers to participate in the procurement and for the municipality to obtain the most favorable and advantageous price. Brand names must only be indicated in the contract and the Purchase Order in order to determine whether the supplier performs or delivers in accordance with the specifications.

11.6 We recommended that the LCE implement the PPAs funded under the 20% DF and require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to monitor and ensure full implementation of the PPAs so that the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes will be attained by the LGU.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a)
MSWDO to continue the proper implementation of the guidelines in the distribution
of emergency subsidy and periodically update the database of the qualified
beneficiaries; and (b) Municipal Accountant (MA) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to
continue to properly utilize, disburse, liquidate, and record funds received from the
National Government.

2.4 We recommended that the LCE require the MT to continue to comply with
the deposit of collections intact and on a daily basis in accordance with Section 32 of
the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

3.9 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Supply Officer to maintain SCs
and submit to the MA the SSMI supported with RIS for recording of issued
inventories for the month; and (b) MA to maintain SLCs to support the controlling
Inventory accounts.

4.16 We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the (a) Municipal
Assessor and MT, as the GSO, to expedite the processing of the land titles on four
parcels of land purchased by the Municipality in CYs 2018 and 2020 and one parcel
of land donated in CY 2021; (b) MT to (i) immediately request for disposal of
unserviceable PPE to prevent their further deterioration and decline in value; and
(ii)to prepare and submit the RPCPPE; (c) MA to (i) derecognize from the books the
unserviceable PPE; and (ii) recognize in the books the donated parcel of land; and
(d) MT and MA to maintain complete PCs and PPELCs, respectively, for easy
reconciliation of records.

5.5 We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-MENRO to (a) strictly
enforce the sanctions on violators of the Ordinances pertaining to solid waste
management; (b) intensify and involve the barangay governments in monitoring the
compliance of constituents with environmental laws, rules and ordinances; and (c)
continue to properly inform and encourage the constituents on the proper segregation
of waste.

6.8 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer’s Office and
Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to properly identify, supervise
and closely monitor the timely and effective implementation of the development
projects under the 20% DF.

7.7 We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to (a) continue to comply
with the proper allocation of the required budget for GAD, preparation of GPB, full
execution of GAD PPAs and the timely submission of the GAD AR; and (b) restate in
the succeeding year’s GPB all the GAD PPAs that were not implemented in CY 2021
due to the pandemic.

8.8 We recommended that the LCE (a) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for
the LDRRMF and optimize its utilization in accordance with the guidelines on the use
thereof; (b) direct the MDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to maintain the
preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified
correct by the MA and submitted to the AT of the LGU on or before the 15th day after
the end of each month; (c) require the MA to support all disbursement vouchers with
complete documentation and proper approval before payment is made; and (d)
require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to submit to the AT the
lacking documents on transactions totaling ₱157,216.69.

9.4 We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to continue to comply
with the remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR intact and within the prescribed
period.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to comply
with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations
including the Municipal share within the prescribed period.

11.4 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to comply
with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan
amortizations within the prescribed period.

12.4 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to comply
with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within
the prescribed period.

13.4 We recommended that the LCE require the persons liable to immediately
settle the suspensions.

observations.managementAction: Inventory taking was conducted for the year 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the breakdown/composition of the amount due the Provincial Government.  If necessary, reconciliation with the records of the Province should be made to account for the difference and to facilitate the drawing of an adjusting entry, where necessary, to ensure the accuracy of the account.
observations.managementAction: The ICO Municipal Accountant said that the Municipal Accountant tried to verify the difference between the amounts recorded in the books of the Municipality and the records of the Province.
observations.reason: The amount due to the Provincial Government recorded in the books as of December 31, 2016 did not tally with the records of the Province.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the employees concerned strictly observe the timely submission of financial reports and accounts to facilitate the timely verification and review thereon and to avoid the imposition of sanctions as duly provided under Section 39 (1) (3) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Trial Balances were prepared and submitted to the Office. The Management said that they will try their best to submit all accounts before deadline on 2017.
observations.reason: Monthly trial balances and some accounts were still submitted late to the Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Treasury Department and all personnel engaged or related to the generation and collection of income employ their best efforts in achieving the targeted income of the Municipality to ensure full implementation of projects/programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management said that they are exerting their best efforts to achieve target income.
observations.reason: There was again a negative variance between the target and actual income for 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all claims against the funds of the Municipality are in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before certifying as to the completeness of supporting documents in the Disbursement Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The ICO- Municipal Accountant did not know some of the required documents and said that they will refer to COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.reason: Lacking documents noted on 2015 transactions were no longer noted in most of the transactions in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper recording of supplies and materials purchased by the LGU to ensure the accuracy of inventory and expense accounts.  Moreover, the General Services Officer (GSO) or the ICO-Municipal Treasurer (if the LGU has no designated GSO) should maintain stock cards, issue supplies using the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and consolidate issuances using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The Municipal Accountant shall likewise maintain supplies ledger cards for each stock for reconciliation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Supplies and materials were recorded as inventory upon purchase.
observations.reason: Inventory accounts maintained in the books in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended strict compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016, there is an unliquidated cash advance for supplementary feeding.
observations.reason: The said cash advance is already properly accounted for in the books as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of paying reimbursements but in cases where direct payment is not really possible, the Municipal Accountant should refrain from withholding taxes since the employees who make reimbursements are neither suppliers nor dealers.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements for purchases were made only on emergency cases.
observations.reason: The number of reimbursements for purchases decreased in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee see to it that Performance Securities are posted within the required time to ensure the validity of contracts for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: BAC sees to it that Performance Securities are furnished on or before contract signing.
observations.reason: Performance securities for projects in 2016 were posted on or before contract signing.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Officials and employees concerned make sure that the Municipal Government will only grant financial assistance to NGOs/POs that meet all the criteria provided in COA Circular Nos. 95-003 and 2007-001 dated February 15, 1995 and October 25, 2007, respectively and see to it that the amount to be granted shall be duly provided in the WFP and budget of the Municipality.  We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to review the documentary requirements as set forth in the Circulars mentioned, including COA Circular No. 2012-001 (Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions).
observations.managementAction: BAC has reviewed this COA Circular and will comply with its provisions when financial assistance will be granted again to qualified NGO/ PO.
observations.reason: The LGU did not grant financial assistance to any NGO/ PO for the calendar year 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees facilitate the immediate completion of the projects to enable the constituents of the Municipal Government to enjoy the benefits they could derive from the said projects.
observations.managementAction: The LGU have not yet received the funds to finance the completion of the said projects.
observations.reason: There are still some projects of the Municipality that remained unfinished to date.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all LGU officials and employees who are responsible for the preparation and implementation/utilization of the 20% Development Fund see to it that all programmed projects are fully implemented and usage of the fund should be maximized only for purposes for which it was established.
observations.managementAction: For the calendar year 2016, only 63.25% of the 20% Development Fund was utilized but the LGU has actually implemented 8 projects out of 11 which is 72.73%.
observations.reason: Some of the expenses that were charged are not priority uses of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) incorporate in the GAD Plan and Budget activities that are specifically gender and development focused and ensure that these are effectively and efficiently implemented.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of GAD fund increased from 28.10% on 2015 to 37.70% on 2016.
observations.reason: The expenses that were charged to GAD Fund of 2016 were found not specifically addressing gender issues.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and ICO - Municipal Treasurer to continue their good practices of withholding taxes and remitting the same on time in support to the national government’s extensive tax campaign.
observations.managementAction: Management continued their good practice of withholding and remitting taxes.
observations.reason: Proper taxes were deducted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant and timely remittances were made during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) create an inventory committee; (b) conduct the annual physical count of all PPE items and submit the inventory report thereon; (c) prepare and maintain property cards, PPELCs and schedules for the PPE; and (d) strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory results.
observations.managementAction:
An Inventory committee was created in November 2016.

The accounting office started maintaining PPELCs and schedules of the PPE for the year 2016. As to the prior years, management  started  retrieving documents for  PPE procurement.
 
The physical count of PPE will be conducted on the 1st quarter of 2017.
observations.reason: Change of Administration

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) advise the Local Finance Committee and other officials concerned to regularly monitor the Municipality’s cash position; (b) ensure that trust liabilities are used solely for their intended purpose; (c) incur expenditures only for projects, programs, and activities that are highly necessary and cannot be dispensed with; and (d) undertake revenue-enhancing strategies and cost-cutting measures to eliminate totally the cash deficit.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer started separating funds for the 2016 20% development fund & LDRRM funds from the other general fund account. Also, management started the prompt remittance of  obligations to the Provincial Government of Bulacan. 

The MTO is conducting tax campaign in different schools and barangays

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances as of year-end to immediately settle the cash advances;(b) refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unsettled balances;  (c) advise the officials concerned that settlement be made within the prescribed period for proper accounting thereof in the books and to prevent misuse of funds; (d) require every Accountable Officer to be properly bonded before granting of cash advance; (e) stop the practice of granting cash advances for purposes other than the official travels to elected officials; and (f) see to it that only duly appointed or designated disbursing officers may perform disbursing functions.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances totaling P8,031,042.21 were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office and Supplies Custodian in ensuring that (a) the perpetual inventory method is observed in the accounting for inventories; (b) the required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports are prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories; and (c) periodic physical count of inventory on hand is conducted to pave the way for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or  inventory records for proper valuation.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office required all departments to submit the inventory of office supplies as of December 31, 2016 and subsequently adjust the office supplies expense account.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no Supplies Custodian.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require to (a) furnish the audit team copies of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance thereof, together with the bid documents and other pertinent documents; and (b) submit notices of deliveries within the prescribed period to provide the opportunity for the Team to inspect the quality and compliance of the delivered items with the specifications.
observations.managementAction:
The municipal mayor assigned a personnel who will handle the Purchase Orders and  submit them to the Auditor’s office. The POs were submitted late and upon checking, the POs were incomplete and not properly numbered.

Procurement thru bidding was done in December, 2016. All documents are due for submission to COA.
observations.reason: Lack of accounting office personnel that will handle the monitoring of procurement  documents to be submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that appropriations are available before obligations are incurred and that all expenditures which could not be paid in the year they were incurred should be recognized as Accounts Payable before year-end. Moreover, Management should comply with the modified accrual basis of accounting for complete reporting of all financial transactions and obligate all valid claims which were unpaid during the year for fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: All procurements for the year 2016 were recognized as Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) consider appointing another competent official, other than the Municipal Engineer, to head the BAC, pursuant to the rotation policy under Section 11 of RA No. 9184; and (b) ensure that critical activities in the procurement of major projects pass thru the review of other competent persons other than the initiator/major implementer for check and balance.
observations.managementAction: The management  created a new set of BAC members headed by the Municipal Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by transferring the unexpended balance of every year’s budget in LDRRF to the special trust fund created solely to support risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality for the next five years and for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The unspent portion of DRRMF from 2013 to 2015 were  transferred to the Trust Fund account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management (a) strictly comply with the provisions of Section 100 of P.D. 1445 on the prompt submission of accounts to the audit team; (b) enjoin all department heads concerned to commit and render extra time to complete and submit all financial reports and transaction records within the prescribed time, otherwise, withhold monies due the official/employee concerned until they have complied with the submission of the required reports, pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445;  (c) assign competent personnel to handle processing of documents and submission of the same to the respective offices; and (d) effect the immediate submission of all missing /unsubmitted vouchers to the Auditor’s Office for post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) instruct the officials in-charge of the development projects to supervise and monitor closely the timely and effective implementation of the development projects under their respective offices; and (b) ensure that any amendments or revisions in the AIP and/or budget are covered with the proper documentation and authority.
observations.managementAction:
The 2015 projects for the 20% development fund were not fully implemented by the previous administration. The Municipal Engineer resigned last June 25, 2016 and no pertinent records were left as to the status of the 2015 20% Development Fund.  When the new Municipal Engineer assumed office, he concentrated on the implementation of the 2016 projects. 
The new management has created new set of BAC members effective July,  2016. Registration with PHILGEPS was done in Sept. 2017. Partial bidding of projects for the 20% development fund (2016) was done in December 2016.
observations.reason: Change of Administration

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) convene the Local Solid Waste Management Board for the formulation and execution of the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP); (b) strictly implement the anti-littering ordinance and enforce sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management; and (c) conduct seminars and/or trainings to enhance public’s awareness on solid waste management.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Solid Waste Management Board was formulated on October 10, 2016. The Board has  submitted the ten year Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR but without a WACS yet.  
As of the fourth quarter of 2016, there were only six Barangays that are compliant on waste segregation and only one barangay has MRF.
The Information Education Campaign  scheduled this first quarter of 2017.
observations.reason:
Absence of   Officer that will fully handle the functions of the Office.

 The personnel hired by the municipality last July 2016 has resigned. The Municipal Engineer is the current OIC in the office.
